purpose: Explicit public-launch manifest for root HTML pages built by Vite. A root *.html file ships only after it is listed here with publicLaunchIntent.

pages.publicLaunchIntent: Primary Sundog public website entry point.
Public lab positioning, philosophy, origin bridge, and claim boundaries.
Public alignment and Bayes comparator primer defining route fidelity, same-information baselines, and current claim-hygiene ledger.
Public gallery of application surfaces and evidence tiers.
Public Balance workbench interface.
Public Ask Sundog claim-boundary experiment page.
Public h-of-x math workbench: plain first-equation demo for the promoted parhelion-offset inverse and its eligibility boundary.
Public geometry workbench shelf: cap-set primer, halo/h-of-x routing, and bounded apparatus framing for discrete-geometry discoveries.
Public vocabulary legend/reference page for halo terms and site terms; distinct from the visual Halo Atlas.
Public Mesa operating-envelope and traceability page.
Public Pressure Mines workbench interface.
Public origin and provenance story.
Public human and agent navigation map for evidence pages, docs, workbenches, and source context.
Public paper-theme design primitive demo linked from docs.
Public structural-failure boundary-map page: frozen falsifier, closed-form regime-separability, rendered Cut-3 held.
Legacy public alias that redirects /atlas or /atlas.html to the canonical visual Halo Atlas at /sundog.
Canonical public Sundog geometry and visual Halo Atlas page; distinct from the vocabulary Legend.
Public Sundog workbench interface.
Public Three-Body workbench interface.
Public cap-set workbench: interactive F_3^n exploration framing the 2016 polynomial-method breakthrough as a precedent for the 2026 unit-distance disproof.
Public unit-distance overlay: plain-English read of OpenAI's 2026 disproof, from cyclic cubic field through unramified pro-3 tower to CM lattice projecting to many unit-distance pairs.

Public Isotrophy audit-chain page: preserves the K_facet v0.3h pillar (20 structural-zero receipts plus one O_617 quarantine) and stages the v0.3-v0.11 conditional-rank profile: v0.7 velocity-fraction positive, v0.10b mass-marginal held-out null, and v0.11 within-m3 conditional-rank pass on the analyzable supplementary-B rows.

SKETCH — method-essay bridge between the Shadow Faraday Branch A receipt and three load-bearing translations to AI behavioral guarantees (local gauge-invariant readout, structural zero, named quarantine). Class B candidate in the SEO matrix: local Bucket 1 artifacts are now present, including bespoke 1200x630 og:image at public/og/safety-method.png, full OG/Twitter/JSON-LD metadata, sitemap entry, clean-url redirect, and inbound thread from index.html through /faraday to /safety-method.html. Post-deploy validator pass and the staged compander citation ratchet remain owed before /safety-method is externally pushed. The page is indexable (no noindex) but flagged in-product as SKETCH so claim hygiene is preserved while the translation is evaluated. Anchored to docs/faraday/SHADOW_FARADAY.md#phase-5-chapter-close as the worked example; explicitly not a proof of AI safety, not a theory of interpretability, and not a recipe.

Public Shadow Faraday Zero-Out evidence page. Experiment chapter is closed: Phases 1-5 receipts cross-linked at docs/faraday/SHADOW_FARADAY.md (with Branch A Phase 3 in docs/faraday/FARADAY_PHASE3_DERIVATIONS.md, 5/5 Phase 4 battery in docs/faraday/FARADAY_PHASE4_VERIFICATION.md via npm run faraday:phase4, and Phase 5 chapter close at docs/faraday/SHADOW_FARADAY.md#phase-5-chapter-close with receipts catalog, scope limitations, extension menu, and fidelity audit). Bucket 1 local artifacts are present: designed 1200x630 og:image at public/og/faraday.png, full OG/Twitter metadata, JSON-LD TechArticle, sitemap entry, clean-url redirect, index.html internal link, and onward bridge to /safety-method. Post-deploy LinkedIn/Twitter validator pass remains to record after deployment.

Public Navier-Stokes C1 evidence page: deep dive for the Reading-2 regime-2 witness that now replicates at G=200 and G=300 under a portable objective. State-insufficient AND control-sufficient witness, dimension-robust (d=32, d=18), 942,834 G=300 witness pairs, composed at matching epsilon_K. Standing claim boundary: this is a witness, not a Navier-Stokes existence or smoothness claim, not a Clay-problem-adjacent claim. Review-gated; C1 remains UNPROMOTED until an external PDE reviewer pass closes promotion criterion (c). Inbound from generality.html umbrella and from the seventh load-bearing pillar on the homepage. Bespoke OG card lives at public/og/navierstokes.png.

Public P-vs-NP verifier evidence page: a spectacle replay of the bounded alignment-verification arc. Act I, the v6 op-count certificate is cheaper to check (527,297 ops) than the policy was to find (555,876 ops), ratio 0.948587, safety-complete in the toy envelope. Act II, transplanted to the mesa bridge the certificate is spoofed by a single source-bound seed block whose mean drifted to signature 0.23545 over the frozen 0.23 accept line, at 6.25% of the attacker budget. Act III, a frozen 3-of-4 block-consensus repair closes the spoof but quarantines on a block-unstable disclosure flag of a protected cell. Standing claim boundary, stated above the fold: bounded alignment-verification borrowing the finding-vs-checking asymmetry as vocabulary, NOT the Millennium P-vs-NP problem, NOT a complexity-theoretic result, op-count not wall-clock, mesa-local. Every number replays a filed receipt. Inbound from the generality.html umbrella P-vs-NP row. Companion terminal demo at scripts/pvnp-verifier-spoofer-demo.mjs and digest at docs/pvnp/V6_CERTIFICATE_DIGEST.md.

Public High-Stakes Generality umbrella: transfer-test matrix across six lanes — Navier-Stokes C1 (review-gated witness, strongest current lane, deep dive at /navierstokes), Riemann (3 lanes 3 bounded nulls, review-gated), Yang-Mills (4 probes 1 bounded null, review-gated), P-vs-NP (safety green / cost-hold), ARC-AGI (execution-hold after sparse absolute fibers and oracle-leakage findings), and Three-Body Phase 15C (running, 6/12 shards logged). Carries the asymmetric framing explicitly — NSE has its own page, the other five are honest cards on the matrix. Links the generality layer back to /h-of-x, /geometry, /alignment, /legend, and /repo-map as the site route from first equation to harder transfer tests. Not a Clay-problem claims page; not a theorem-claims page. Bespoke OG card lives at public/og/generality.png.

REVIEW-ONLY reviewer surface, NOT a public launch. Finite-field Kakeya reader + tiny F_q^2 workbench + body-resistance framing; Sundog claims NO Kakeya result, no finite-field->Euclidean transfer, nothing about open n>=4, and explicitly fences it as NOT a regime-2/control-sufficiency witness. Deployed unlinked at /kakeya with a visible NOT PEER REVIEWED banner and noindex; NO public inbound path from index/legend/nav and excluded from the sitemap until an external finite-field-incidence sanity check clears it (Bucket 1 + sign-off). Interactive surfaces at /kakeya/workbench.html and /kakeya/gallery.html are also unlinked. Packet: docs/kakeya/EXTERNAL_REVIEW_PACKET.md.
